content.problemStatement:
‚ÄúJesse, you asked me if I was in the meth business, or the money business‚Ä¶ Neither. I‚Äôm in the empire business.‚Äù

Walter‚Äôs sold his stack in Gray Matter Technologies, a company which he deserved half a credit, for peanuts. Now this company is worth a billion dollar company. Walter wants to get it's shares to have his Empire Business back and he founds an opportunity.

There are NNN persons having shares A1,A2,A3,‚Ä¶ANA_1, A_2, A_3, ‚Ä¶ A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,A3‚Äã,‚Ä¶AN‚Äã in this company. Walter can buy these shares with their minimum **Sold Values**.

**Sold Values** of a person's share iii (1‚â§i‚â§N)(1 \leq i \leq N) (1‚â§i‚â§N) with another person's share jjj (1‚â§j‚â§N)(1 \leq j \leq N)(1‚â§j‚â§N) is equal to Aj+‚à£i‚àíj‚à£A_j+|i-j|Aj‚Äã+‚à£i‚àíj‚à£. So, a person's share can have NNN possible sold values and Walter has to find minimum sold value among them for each person.

Since Walter has to run his meth business also he asks you to find minimum sold value for each person.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the testcases follow.
The First line of each test case contains a integer NNN.
The Second line of each test case contains NNN space integers namely A1,A2,‚Ä¶ANA_1,A_2,‚Ä¶A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶AN‚Äã.
###Output:
For each test case, output in single line NNN space integers denoting minimum sold value for each person.

###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§1051 \leq T \leq 10^51‚â§T‚â§105
1‚â§N‚â§2‚àó1061 \leq N \leq 2*10^6 1‚â§N‚â§2‚àó106
1‚â§Ai‚â§1091 \leq A_i \leq 10^9 1‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§109
Sum of NNN over all test cases will not exceed 2‚àó1062*10^62‚àó106.
